sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, dated December 15, 1930
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel reports on the success of Hans Weisse’s lectures at the Prussian Ministry of Culture in Berlin, where he also met Moriz Violin and Anthony van Hoboken; the latter told him he did not want to bear the full cost of a new edition of the music of C. P. E. Bach. — Oppel will be giving a series of lectures on Schenker’s theory to a ladies’ club in Leipzig in the new year.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for December 16, 1930: “Von Oppel (Br.): über Weisses Vortrag; macht ein + über v. H.; über seinen eigenen Vortrag im Conservatorium, in dem er auch mich zu propagiren gedenkt!” (“From Oppel (letter): concerning Weisse's lecture; he puts a cross above Hoboken; concerning his own lecture at the Conservatory, in which he also plans to propagandize for me!”).

tei:div.tei:p:
Weisse’s beide VorträgeAt the invitation of Leo Kestenberg, and on the recommendation of Wilhelm Furtwängler, Hans Weisse delivered two lectures on Schenker’s theory at the Prussian Ministry of Education in early December 1930. waren ausgezeichnet, inhaltlich u. formell klar. Die Beispiele waren sehr gut gewählt. Ich hatte grosse Freude und wirkliche Befriedigung. Schade war nur, dass beim 1. Vortrag so wenig Menschen da waren, die ganze musik. Facultät der Universität Berlin glänzte durch Abwesenheit. Aber das hatte ich von den Herren nicht anders erwartet. Weisse spielte mir am andern Tage noch die Bagatellen vor u. die 2st. Psalmen. Seine Kompositionsweise ist mir sehr sympathisch. – Leider konnte
ich zur Discussion über den Contrapunktvortrag nicht dableiben, da ich Donnerstag früh schon wieder hier unterrichten musste. Es kommt ja bei solchen Disc. auch nie viel heraus, da die Gegner ja keine Ahnung vom Umfang u. Art des Stoffes haben. –

Prof Violin bestellte mir Ihre lieben Grüsse. Ich werde ihn in Hamburg einmal aufsuchen. Van Hobocken sprach von der Ph. E. Bach-Ausgabe:A collected edition of the works of Carl Philipp Emanuel Bach, which was discussed intensively in 1930 by the committee overseeing the Photogram Archive at the Austrian National Library; it was eventually abandoned, in part because Schenker did not want to be saddled with heavy editorial responsibilities, in part because Hoboken was unwilling to finance the project fully. er allein könne die Kosten nicht tragen. Er hat gut reden und leben. Ein wirkliches Verantwortungsgefühl aus seinem Reichtum heraus der Kunst gegenüber
hat er noch immer nicht, und wird es auch nie bekommen. –

Jedenfalls aber ist Furtwänglers Schritt eine weitere Etappe zur Verbreitung Ihrer Lehre, und Weisse der berufener Vertreter, der seine Aufgabe glänzend gelöst hat.Hans Weisse was to move to New York City the following year, where he taught at the Mannes Music School and Columbia University until his death in 1940, so transmitting Schenker’s theory to North America, and ultimately realizing Oppel’s prediction on an international scale. Langsam aber sicher wird die Wahrheit durchdringen.

Im Januar und Februar halte ich hier 6 Vorlesungen, in denen ich Sie ordentlich propagieren werde. Es geschieht in einem Zirkel von Damen, die sonst nur Universitätsprofessoren über Litteratur, Malerei etc. reden lassen. Allenfalls Höchstens einmal über Wagner sonst. Ich habe aber bei der Vorstandsdame durchgesetzt, dass auch einmal musiktheoretische Dinge behandelt werden.
Straube’s Frau wird auch teilnehmen, die wird sich dann ja manchmal wundern u. I ihrem Mann berichten.Oppel gave his lectures between mid-January and February 17, 1931: see OJ 13/10, [121], January 17, [122], January 23, [123], February 3, and [124], February 19, 1931. –

Weisse’s two lectures were outstanding, clear both in content and form. The examples were very well chosen; I took great pleasure and genuine satisfaction from them. It was just a pity that so few people attended the first lecture; the entire music faculty of the University of Berlin was conspicuous in its absence. But I had not expected otherwise of the gentlemen. The next day Weisse also played me his bagatelles and two-voice psalm settings. I find his compositional style very agreeable. – Unfortunately,
I could not stay for the discussion of the lecture on counterpoint, since I had to teach here already on Thursday morning. But such discussions never produce much, since the opponents have no idea whatever of the nature and scope of the materials. –

Prof Violin conveyed your kind greetings to me. I shall look him up in Hamburg some time. Hoboken spoke about the C. P. E. Bach edition: he cannot bear the costs of it on his own. He speaks and lives well. A true sense of responsibility with respect to art, in consideration of his wealth,
is something he still does not have, nor will he ever acquire it. –

In any event, Furtwängler’s effort represents a further stage in the dissemination of your theory, and Weisse is its desigated advocate, who has fulfilled his mission splendidly. Slowly but surely the truth will prevail.

In January and February, I shall give six lectures here in which I shall promote your work thoroughly. It will take place in a circle of ladies, who are usually addressed only by university professors about literature, painting, and the like. Or, in other instances, about Wagner at most. But I managed to persuade the chairlady that even matters of music theory should be discussed for once.
Straube’s wife will also be attending; she will then be in for a surprise and can report to her husband.

tei:closer.tei:salute: With a cordial greeting to the two of you,
